pi_items.source: setup_nx_witness.sh line 199: CAMERA_NAMES array defines 'Entrance Camera' as the second camera to be created and renamed. Lines 218-222 perform the renaming via API PATCH call.
task.json line 5 description explicitly states: Trigger Name: 'Report Incident'. This is the user-defined soft trigger name that must be created. Verifier.py line 76 validates this exact name.
task.json line 4 description states: Tags: 'Manual_Report'. Verifier.py line 114 validates this tag is present: tag_ok = any(expected_bookmark_tag in t for t in tags).

task.json line 3 description states: 'Fixed Duration: 1 minute (60 seconds)'. 60 seconds × 1000 milliseconds/second = 60,000 ms. Verifier.py line 113 allows ±10% tolerance (±6000ms). Verified correct conversion.

task.json line 5 description states: 'Duration: 5 minutes (300 seconds)'. 300 seconds × 1000 milliseconds/second = 300,000 ms. Verifier.py line 126 allows ±10% tolerance (±30000ms). Verified correct conversion.

task.json description specifies two required actions: Action 1 = Create a Bookmark, Action 2 = Start Recording. Verifier.py lines 105 and 123 check for these action types: ['bookmark', 'bookmarkLog'] and ['recording', 'cameraRecording'].

privileged_info_summary: The task configures a compound soft trigger named 'Report Incident' on the 'Entrance Camera' in an Nx Witness VMS environment. The trigger executes two simultaneous actions: (1) creates a bookmark with 60-second duration tagged 'Manual_Report', and (2) starts a 5-minute (300-second) recording. The environment uses synthetic data with virtual test cameras created and named during setup. All metadata values are correct and match the task requirements and implementation constraints.
